transformation_type: [3+2] annulation of trimethylsilylallenes with electron-deficient alkenes to cyclopentenes
mechanism_type: Lewis-acid-activated allylic cation capture with 1,2-silyl shift
common_conditions: treat electron-deficient alkene with trimethylsilylallene in the presence of TiCl4 at low temperature, then hydrolyze to give substituted cyclopentene
description_short: TiCl4-promoted [3+2] annulation of trimethylsilylallenes with activated alkenes to furnish highly substituted cyclopentenes.

transformation_type: electron-rich heterodiene [4+2] cycloaddition to oxygenated cyclohexenes or dihydropyrones
mechanism_type: hetero Diels–Alder / Lewis-acid-assisted Mukaiyama-aldol cyclization manifold
common_conditions: combine Danishefsky diene with aldehyde, ketone, quinone, or imine partner under Lewis-acid or thermal conditions to generate oxygenated six-membered rings
description_short: Cycloaddition chemistry of Danishefsky's diene that rapidly builds oxygenated or heteroatom-containing six-membered rings from carbonyl, quinone, or imine partners.

transformation_type: alpha-halo carbonyl compound plus aldehyde/ketone to epoxide (glycidic ester)
mechanism_type: base-promoted aldol addition followed by intramolecular SN2 epoxide closure
common_conditions: deprotonate alpha-halo ester or related pronucleophile with base, add aldehyde or ketone, then allow intramolecular ring closure to glycidic ester
description_short: Base-mediated condensation of aldehydes or ketones with alpha-halo carbonyl pronucleophiles to form glycidic esters and related epoxides.

transformation_type: enolate or heteroatom oxidation by N-sulfonyloxaziridines
mechanism_type: electrophilic oxygen transfer from oxaziridine to nucleophiles / enolates
common_conditions: generate enolate or expose sulfide to N-sulfonyloxaziridine reagent to deliver oxygen-transfer products under mild neutral conditions
description_short: Oxidation chemistry of N-sulfonyloxaziridines, especially alpha-hydroxylation of enolates and selective oxygen transfer to sulfides or other nucleophiles.

transformation_type: photochemical enone-alkene [2+2] cycloaddition with optional retro-aldol fragmentation
mechanism_type: triplet enone photochemistry via exciplex/1,4-biradical intermediates
common_conditions: irradiate enone with alkene to form cyclobutane adduct, optionally induce fragmentation of the beta-hydroxy ketone to unmask 1,5-dicarbonyl products
description_short: Photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition between enones and alkenes that can furnish cyclobutanes directly or 1,5-diketones after retro-aldol fragmentation.
